report_name: APT-C-60 Targets Japan with 'SpyGlace' Malware, Abusing Trusted Cloud Services

threat_actors.name: APT-C-60
threat_actors.description: A cyber-espionage group targeting organizations in Japan. The group utilizes a multi-stage infection process, abusing legitimate cloud and developer services like Proton Drive, jsDelivr, and GitHub to deliver its SpyGlace malware and evade detection.
threat_actors.goals: Cyber-espionage and data theft against Japanese entities.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: The campaign initiates with spear-phishing emails that contain a link to a malicious file hosted on Proton Drive.

attack_patterns.name: Malicious File
attack_patterns.description: The attack relies on the user executing a malicious .LNK shortcut file contained within a downloaded RAR archive.

attack_patterns.name: System Binary Proxy Execution: Mshta
attack_patterns.description: The legitimate Windows binary mshta.exe is abused to execute embedded JavaScript code from the malicious LNK file, which then fetches the next stage payload.

attack_patterns.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
attack_patterns.description: Malicious payloads and components are downloaded from a chain of trusted online services, including Proton Drive, jsDelivr, GitHub, GitLab, and Codeberg.

attack_patterns.name: Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: The malware uses standard HTTPS traffic to communicate with legitimate services like GitHub and jsDelivr for payload delivery, blending in with normal web activity to evade network detection.

malware.name: SpyGlace
malware.description: A backdoor used for espionage and data theft, identified in versions v3.1.15, v3.1.17, and v3.1.18. It is the final payload deployed by APT-C-60 in a multi-stage infection chain that abuses trusted online services.

tools.name: mshta.exe
tools.description: Abused to execute JavaScript code from a malicious LNK file to fetch the next stage of the malware from jsDelivr.

tools.name: git.exe
tools.description: A legitimate git binary, likely bundled with the malware, is used to connect to repositories on GitHub, GitLab, or Codeberg to download and assemble the final SpyGlace payload.

identities.name: Organizations in Japan
identities.description: Unspecified organizations in Japan targeted by the APT-C-60 cyber-espionage campaign.

infrastructure.name: Trusted Service Payload Hosting
infrastructure.description: APT-C-60 abused a chain of legitimate services including Proton Drive, jsDelivr, GitHub, GitLab, and Codeberg to host malicious files, stage payloads, and blend C2 traffic.

indicators.name: Suspicious Mshta.exe Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Mshta.exe Execution by LNK File
id: 2b3c1a9e-8d7f-4c6a-b5e4-f3a2b1c0d9e8
status: experimental
description: Detects mshta.exe being launched with a command line, potentially from a malicious LNK file, as seen in the APT-C-60 SpyGlace campaign.
references:
  - https://mallory.ai/stories/019f59e0-245f-7119-b412-fd2d64c58eda
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-13
tags:
  - attack.t1218.005
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\mshta.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'javascript:'
      - '.hta'
  filter_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\explorer.exe'
      - '\OUTLOOK.EXE'
      - '\WINWORD.EXE'
      - '\EXCEL.EXE'
  condition: selection and filter_parent

indicators.name: Anomalous Git Execution for Payload Retrieval
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Git Execution for Payload Retrieval
id: 9e8d7f6a-5c4b-4a3b-2c1d-0e9f8d7c6b5a
status: experimental
description: Detects git.exe running from a non-standard directory, which could indicate its use for downloading malicious payloads as seen in the APT-C-60 campaign.
references:
  - https://mallory.ai/stories/019f59e0-245f-7119-b412-fd2d64c58eda
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-13
tags:
  - attack.t1105
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\git.exe'
  filter_legit_paths:
    Image|startswith:
      - 'C:\Program Files\Git\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\Git\'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit_paths

indicators.name: APT-C-60 Trusted Service Abuse
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: APT-C-60 Trusted Service Abuse
id: f3a2b1c0-d9e8-4b3c-1a9e-8d7f6c5b4a3b
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to legitimate services known to be abused by APT-C-60 for payload delivery. This rule is for hunting and may be noisy.
references:
  - https://mallory.ai/stories/019f59e0-245f-7119-b412-fd2d64c58eda
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-13
tags:
  - attack.t1071.001
level: low
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName:
      - 'jsdelivr.net'
      - 'proton.me'
      - 'codeberg.org'
      - 'github.com'
      - 'gitlab.com'
  condition: selection

relationships.source_name: Anomalous Git Execution for Payload Retrieval
